metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` to the Files section and scope, wiring the `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py` drift gate there too — mirroring exactly how `gen_env_registry.py` is present in both workflows — so the `test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci` parity assertion continues to pass.
findings.finding: The plan wires the drift gate only into `.github/workflows/test.yml` but omits `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`. The repo enforces a hard parity invariant via `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py::test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci`, which asserts that every `scripts/*.py` command found in `test.yml` must also appear in `gerrit-verify.yaml`. Adding `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py` to `test.yml` alone would immediately break `make test` (the parity test detects the asymmetry). The plan's 'Files' section lists `.github/workflows/test.yml` but never mentions `gerrit-verify.yaml`.
findings.location: Scope section — 'Wire a CI drift gate in `.github/workflows/test.yml`'; Files section listing
findings.suggested_fix: Add `gerrit-verify.yaml` to the Files list and include a step in the Scope bullet that mirrors the test.yml gate step. Also add `'cli-reference drift gate': 'scripts/gen_cli_reference.py'` to `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` in `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` (or the generator can be run in the same step as the existing env-registry gate to avoid a new named step).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `rebar enrich --help` produces parseable help output (it currently has no argparse/--help handler in `cmd_enrich`); if not, the plan must specify a fallback strategy (e.g., a hardcoded docstring entry, adding `--help` support to `cmd_enrich`, or exempting it from subprocess capture).
findings.finding: `rebar enrich --help` will not produce a help page: `cmd_enrich` (enrich_drain.py:303) has no argparse and no --help guard, so the subprocess capture in the generator will attempt a live drain(), require a tracker directory, and fail — producing no usable help content and likely a non-zero exit code.
findings.location: Scope section — 'family 2 via the explicit intercept list + `rebar <cmd> --help`'; Source of truth section — 'captures each via `rebar <cmd> --help` (subprocess)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `if '--help' in argv or '-h' in argv:` guard to `cmd_enrich` that prints usage to stdout and returns 0, OR document in the generator that `enrich` is a special case using a hardcoded usage string drawn from `cmd_enrich.__doc__`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies `.github/workflows/test.yml` contains the CLI-reference drift-gate step (e.g., `grep -q 'gen_cli_reference' .github/workflows/test.yml` or inspect the job definition), so the CI wiring is a testable deliverable and not just implied.
findings.finding: The `.github/workflows/test.yml` modification is listed as a deliverable in both Scope and Files, but no acceptance criterion verifies that the CI drift gate was actually wired into the workflow file.
findings.location: Scope (third bullet), Files list, Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] CI gate is wired: `.github/workflows/test.yml` contains a step that runs `python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py --check` (or equivalent `git diff --exit-code -- docs/cli-reference.md` after regeneration), confirmed by `grep -q gen_cli_reference .github/workflows/test.yml`.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error-handling behavior for `rebar <cmd> --help` subprocess calls: define what the generator should do (exit with a clear message, which exit code) when a subcommand is not found, returns non-zero, or times out, and add a subprocess timeout to prevent CI hangs.
findings.finding: The plan specifies subprocess invocation of `rebar <cmd> --help` for the 16 intercept-arm commands but does not specify error-handling behavior when a subprocess call fails (e.g., `rebar` not on PATH in the environment, non-zero exit from a command, or timeout). Without specified handling, the generator could crash with an opaque exception, silently emit empty/partial help blocks, or produce a misleading generated file, all with no actionable error message for the developer.
findings.location: Scope — 'family 2 via the explicit intercept list + `rebar <cmd> --help`' and AC completeness proof
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'Subprocess calls use a timeout (e.g. 10 s); on non-zero exit or timeout the generator exits 1 with a message naming the failing command. If `rebar` is not on PATH, exit 1 with "rebar must be installed (pip install -e .)". These error paths are exercised by the completeness-proof AC step.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` and `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` (update `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES`) to the Files section and Scope, mirroring the exact pattern used for `gen_env_registry.py`.
findings.finding: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` and `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` are unenumerated consumers that MUST be updated when the new drift gate is wired into `test.yml`. The repo enforces (via `test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci`) that every `scripts/*.py` invoked in `test.yml` also appears in `gerrit-verify.yaml`, and (`test_verified_gate_runs_every_shared_gate`) that every gate signature in `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` appears in both files. Adding `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py` to `test.yml` without mirroring it into `gerrit-verify.yaml` and adding its signature to `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` will immediately break both parity tests.
findings.suggested_fix: In `Files`, add `gerrit-verify.yaml` (mirror the new CLI-reference drift gate step) and `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` (add `"cli-reference drift gate": "scripts/gen_cli_reference.py"` to `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES`). In `Scope`, note that the CI gate must be wired into both `test.yml` AND `gerrit-verify.yaml`, exactly as the env-registry gate was.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief note to CLAUDE.md (or a docs/design doc) explaining the dual-family CLI surface (help-backed vs. intercept-arm commands), the generator's explicit intercept list, and the expected workflow when adding a new command to either family.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel dual-family CLI doc-generation pattern (explicit intercept list in generator + drift check against _cli/__init__.py source), but does not update CLAUDE.md or any developer-facing guide explaining: (a) that two disjoint command families exist and why, (b) how to add a new intercept command (update the generator's list + the drift check will catch omissions), or (c) how to add a new help-backed subcommand. A developer encountering this for the first time has no written guidance beyond reading the generator script itself.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `CLAUDE.md` entry (or extend the existing developer guide) with a section titled 'CLI Reference Generation' covering: (1) the two command families and why they exist, (2) how to add a new help-backed subcommand (add a .txt file under rebar/_cli/help/), (3) how to add a new intercept command (add to _cli/__init__.py ladder AND update the list in scripts/gen_cli_reference.py), and (4) how to run the drift gate locally (python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py --check).

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`' for deriving command names, usage strings, and flags, but the actual source of truth is `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt` (static package-data text files) plus dispatch frozensets in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`. No structured CLI registry exists in `_commands`.
findings.location: What section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the plan to say: 'derive the command inventory from the dispatch frozensets in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` and the static help text in `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt`; for commands with native argparse parsers (review, llm, workflow, etc.), invoke each with `--help` and capture stdout.' Remove the reference to a CLI registry in `_commands`.

findings.finding: The plan specifies 'single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands' as the data source for the generator, but the actual authoritative command enumeration lives across three locations: (1) src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt (known_subcommands() — the canononical list used by the CLI itself), (2) src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py (intercept arms for reconcile, review, review-code, scan-spec, verify-completion, review-plan, sign-review, enrich, explain, verify-commit-ticket, workflow, llm, jira-onboard, prompt, criteria, config, plus frozenset dispatch groups), and (3) src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py (_REGISTRY + _ARGV_REGISTRY, leaf-write only). A generator targeting only src/rebar/_commands would miss the majority of commands.
findings.location: What section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / --help (single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands' with: 'derive the command list from src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt (the authoritative enumeration) and usage strings from the same package-data files; harvest arity/flag metadata from src/rebar/_commands.__init__._REGISTRY and _ARGV_REGISTRY for the leaf-write subset.'

findings.finding: The plan names 'src/rebar/_commands' as the single source of truth for the command list, but this covers only ~13 of ~50 CLI commands. The actual authoritative source is src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt (consumed by known_subcommands()); an additional ~15+ commands live in src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py intercept arms (reconcile, review, review-plan, workflow, llm, jira-onboard, config, explain, etc.) and read-path frozensets — none of these are in src/rebar/_commands. A generator built against _commands alone would produce an incomplete reference, and AC3 ('Adding/removing a CLI command or flag changes the generated output') would silently fail for any command in the _cli intercept layer.
findings.location: What section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / --help (single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands)'
findings.suggested_fix: Point gen_cli_reference.py at the package data in src/rebar/_cli/help/: enumerate known_subcommands() for the command list, read each <cmd>.txt for the usage string, and read overview.txt for the top-level structure. This gives complete, already-maintained coverage with zero subprocess invocation needed.

findings.finding: The plan asserts a single source of truth in 'src/rebar/_commands' but the actual CLI command surface is split across at least five distinct structures: _REGISTRY, _ARGV_REGISTRY (both in _commands/__init__.py), seven+ frozensets in _cli/__init__.py routing reads/gates/lifecycle/bridge/IO commands, and per-command argparse parsers. The generator design needed to cover the full surface is materially more complex than the env-registry prior art (which had one clean scan target), and the plan does not acknowledge this fragmentation.
findings.location: What section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / --help (single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands)'

findings.finding: The plan names `src/rebar/_commands` as the 'single source of truth' for the command list, but that module only registers ~13 leaf-write commands (`_REGISTRY` + `_ARGV_REGISTRY`). The actual full CLI surface (~50 subcommands) already lives as machine-readable package-data in `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt`, enumerable in-process via `_help.known_subcommands()` and `_help.subcommand_help(sub)`. Specifying `_commands` as the source would cause the generator to rebuild partial command-discovery from scratch while missing the majority of the CLI, when the full, authoritative enumeration already exists and is already used by tests.
findings.location: Plan body: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)'
findings.suggested_fix: In `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py`, use `from rebar._cli._help import known_subcommands, subcommand_help, overview` to enumerate all subcommands and their usage strings. This reuses the existing machine-readable package-data already pinned by golden tests, rather than partially rebuilding command discovery from `_commands`.

findings.finding: The plan's named source of truth — 'CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)' — is materially incomplete. The actual CLI surface spans at least three distinct locations: (1) `_REGISTRY` + `_ARGV_REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py` (covers ~13 leaf-write commands); (2) hard-coded `if argv[0] == <sub>` intercept arms in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (covers `review`, `review-code`, `scan-spec`, `workflow`, `llm`, `reconcile`, `jira-onboard`, `prompt`, `criteria`, `config`, `verify-commit-ticket`, `sign-review`, `explain`, `enrich`, and others — none present in either registry); and (3) static `.txt` package-data files in `src/rebar/_cli/help/` (51 files — the authoritative known-subcommand catalog via `known_subcommands()`). A generator targeting only `src/rebar/_commands` will silently omit the majority of user-visible subcommands from the reference doc.
findings.location: What / Files section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `rebar._cli._help.known_subcommands()` (which reads `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt`) as the command catalog, and read each `<sub>.txt` for the usage string. This is already the complete, maintained, golden-pinned source of truth — the generator becomes a straightforward iteration over the package-data directory, exactly mirroring the env-registry scanner's proven pattern.

findings.finding: AC3 ('Adding/removing a CLI command or flag changes the generated output (verified)') has no proving command. 'Verified' is a hedge that does not specify who verifies it, by what mechanism, or what command produces evidence — an executor cannot confirm this criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Adding/removing a CLI command or flag changes the generated output (verified).'
findings.suggested_fix: AC3 proving command: `echo 'stub' >> src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py && python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py && git diff --exit-code docs/cli-reference.md; echo 'EXPECTED non-zero exit (diff detected)'; git checkout src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py docs/cli-reference.md`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
